summary: Formulation Lock feature testing completed. Found and fixed a bug where formulation_locked was incorrectly returning false for telecallers. All 18 backend tests passed. Frontend UI verified working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED: formulation_locked was being calculated AFTER formulations were stripped from the response for telecallers, causing it to always return false. Fixed by moving the check BEFORE stripping formulations.
All formulation lock features working correctly after fix
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (line ~939-960 - moved formulation lock check before stripping formulations)

seed_data_creation: Created test orders CS-0038 (telecaller's locked order) and multiple test customers/orders for automated tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Formulation Lock feature fully tested and working. Bug was found and fixed in server.py - formulation_locked calculation was happening after formulations were stripped for telecallers. The fix moves the check before stripping. Test order CS-0038 is owned by test_tc_payment and has formulation locked.

rca_of_the_issue.bug_description: formulation_locked field was always returning false for telecaller users even when the order had formulations
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: In get_order() endpoint (server.py line ~939-960), the formulation_locked calculation was happening AFTER formulations were stripped from the response for telecallers. Since telecallers don't see formulation data, by the time the check ran, all formulations were already removed, making has_formulation always false.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Moved the formulation_locked calculation to happen BEFORE stripping formulations from the response. This ensures the lock status is calculated based on actual database data, not the stripped response.
reproduction_steps: 1. Login as admin and create an order
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: The fix ensures formulation_locked is calculated before any data stripping occurs, so all users see the correct lock status regardless of their role's formulation visibility permissions.
